Stroke of Luck.name: Stroke of Luck
Stroke of Luck.description: You have got it when it counts. If your attack misses a target within normal range, you can turn the miss into a hit. Alternatively, if you fail an ability check, you can treat the d20 roll as a 20. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

Stubborn Rage.name: Stubborn Rage
Stubborn Rage.description: Your rage ends only when its duration expires, you fall unconscious, or you choose to end it.

Stunts.name: Stunts
Stunts.description:
As part of your training, you’ve learned to use unique tactics with certain weapons. You gain access to special maneuvers called stunts.

To use a stunt, you must wield a weapon that matches the weapon type listed in the stunt description (if one is listed). You can only use one stunt per turn. You gain access to advanced stunts at higher levels.

All stunts listed are available to you at 3rd level. You can perform stunts a number of times equal to your PB + 1. After that, you must finish a short or long rest to regain expended uses.

Saves. Some stunts require your target to make a save to resist its effects. The save DC for these stunts is calculated as follows:

Stunt Save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus (PB) + your STR or DEX modifier (your choice)

Stunts are presented in alphabetical order:

@Embed[.Advancement.hsVhbQnZQ46oIjug inline]

Subtle Spell.name: Subtle Spell
Subtle Spell.description: When you cast a spell, you can spend 1 sorcery point to cast it without any somatic or verbal components.

utility.condition: When you cast a spell
utility.activationCondition: When you cast a spell

Superior Recovery.name: Superior Recovery
Superior Recovery.description: When you use your Arcane Recovery feature, you can choose to swap out a number of prepared Arcane spells with different Arcane spells from your spellbook—in addition to recovering Arcane spell slots. You can swap out a number of prepared spells equal to half your PB (rounded down) in this way.

Swift Ritual.name: Swift Ritual
Swift Ritual.description: You can cast an Arcane ritual you know as an action, instead of using the ritual’s listed casting time. You must still provide all other components necessary for the casting. Once used, you can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

Tactical Caster.name: Tactical Caster
Tactical Caster.description: When you cast an Arcane spell that causes damage, you can choose yourself and any number of allies you can see to be immune to the damage caused by the spell. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your PB, and you regain all uses of it when you finish a long rest.

utility.condition: When you cast a spell that causes damage
utility.activationCondition: When you cast a spell that causes damage

Tactical Retreat.name: Tactical Retreat
Tactical Retreat.description:
Requires a Melee Weapon

When you hit a creature with a weapon as part of the Attack action, you can immediately move up to half your speed as part of the same action without provoking opportunity attacks.

utility.condition: When you hit a creature with a weapon as part of the Attack action
utility.activationCondition: When you hit a creature with a weapon as part of the Attack action

Technique: Flurry of Blows.name: Technique: Flurry of Blows
Technique: Flurry of Blows.description: Immediately after you use an action on your turn, you can spend 1 technique point to make two unarmed strikes or make one attack with a monk weapon as a bonus action.

utility.condition: Immediately after you use an action on your turn
utility.activationCondition: Immediately after you use an action on your turn

Technique: Patient Defense.name: Technique: Patient Defense
Technique: Patient Defense.description: You can spend 1 technique point to take the Dodge action as a bonus action on your turn.

Technique: Step of the Wind.name: Technique: Step of the Wind
Technique: Step of the Wind.description: You can spend 1 technique point to take the Disengage or Dash action as a bonus action on your turn. When you do so, your jump distance is doubled until the start of your next turn.

Technique: Stunning Strike.name: Technique: Stunning Strike
Technique: Stunning Strike.description: You can interfere with the flow of energy in an opponent’s body. Once per turn, when you successfully hit another creature with an unarmed strike or monk weapon, you can spend 1 technique point to attempt to stun your foe. When you do so, the target takes damage from your attack as normal and then it must succeed on a CON save or be stunned until the end of your next turn. Each time a stunned target takes damage, it can repeat the save, ending the stunned effect on a success.

savingThrow.condition: When you hit with an unarmed strike or monk weapon
savingThrow.activationCondition: When you hit with an unarmed strike or monk weapon

Techniques.name: Techniques
Techniques.description:
Your training gives you access to special techniques that allow you to amplify and manipulate the energy within yourself and the environment. Your ability to use this energy is represented by technique points. Your monk level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Technique Points column of the Monk Progression table.

You can spend these points to fuel various techniques. You start knowing three techniques: Flurry of Blows, Patient Defense, and Step of the Wind. You learn more technique features as you gain levels in this class.

You recover expended technique points when you finish a short or long rest. You must spend at least 30 minutes of the rest meditating to regain your technique points.

Some of your techniques require your target to make a save to resist the technique’s effects. The save DC is calculated as follows:

Technique save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus (PB) + your WIS modifier

Flurry of Blows

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.classes.Item.W0o7x3R3Zbd9PXdh inline]

Patient Defense

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.classes.Item.bSQE1sk96t3Nziid inline]

Step of the Wind

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.classes.Item.IpmFWGEjBogzw2DX inline]

Thief.name: Thief
Thief.description: You have a reputation for quick thinking and quicker fingers. Leave throat-cutting and con-running to lesser scoundrels. You live to liberate treasures held under lock and key. The thrill of bypassing a sealed door, complex trap, or other “impassable” obstacle is the true prize—followed by the payday that comes from whatever you took.
Thief.short: You have a reputation for quick thinking and quicker fingers. Leave throat-cutting and con-running to lesser scoundrels. You live to liberate treasures held under lock and key. The thrill of bypassing a sealed door, complex trap, or other “impassable” obstacle is the true prize—followed by the payday that comes from whatever you took.

Thief of Five Fates.name: Thief of Five Fates
Thief of Five Fates.description: You can cast bane as a 1st-circle spell as often as you wish, without expending a spell slot or material components.

Thief's Reflexes.name: Thief's Reflexes
Thief's Reflexes.description: You have become adept at laying ambushes and quickly escaping danger. Now, you can take two turns during the first round of any combat. You take your first turn at your normal initiative and your second turn at your initiative minus 10. You can’t use this feature if you are surprised.

Thieves' Cant.name: Thieves' Cant
Thieves' Cant.description:
During your rogue training, you learned Thieves’ Cant, a secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code that allows you to hide messages in seemingly normal conversation. Only another creature who knows Thieves’ Cant understands such messages. It takes four times longer to convey a message in Thieves’ Cant than it does to speak the same idea plainly.

In addition, you understand a set of secret signs and symbols used to convey short, simple messages, such as whether an area is dangerous or the territory of a thieves’ guild, whether loot is nearby, or whether people in an area are easy marks or will provide a safe house for thieves on the run.

Timeless Self.name: Timeless Self
Timeless Self.description:
Your spirit sustains you, body and mind. You gain the following benefits:

  • You can’t be magically aged and suffer none of the frailty of old age. You can still die of old age, however.

  • You no longer need food or water.

  • Your ability scores and hit point maximum can’t be lowered or reduced by any means short of a wish spell.

Tranquil Soul.name: Tranquil Soul
Tranquil Soul.description: You have learned the way of war, so you may better walk the world in peace. As an action, you can spend 3 technique points to cast the sanctuary spell on yourself. The spell lasts until the end of your next short or long rest (or it can end early as normal).

Trap Specialist.name: Trap Specialist
Trap Specialist.description:
When you or a creature within 5 feet of you triggers a trap, you can use your reaction to roll an ability check to attempt to disarm the trap. If you are successful, the trap is disarmed and not triggered. If you are unsuccessful, the trap is triggered as normal.

In addition, you can now disarm any magical traps that would typically require the use of the @UUID[Compendium.black-flag.spells.Item.t61I6zrmEDPdOLfw]{dispel magic} spell. When you do so, make an ability check using only your DEX modifier, without adding any other modifiers to the roll. If the result meets or exceeds the trap’s DC (your GM has this number), it is disarmed as if you had successfully cast the dispel magic spell.

utility.condition: When you or a creature within 5 feet of you triggers a trap
utility.activationCondition: When you or a creature within 5 feet of you triggers a trap

Turn the Tide.name: Turn the Tide
Turn the Tide.description: Once on each of your turns, when you hit a creature or object with a weapon attack on your turn, you can cause the attack to deal additional damage (of the same damage type as the weapon) equal to your STR or DEX score (your choice). The attack’s damage ignores resistance and immunity, and it can’t be reduced or avoided by any means.

Extra Damage.name: Extra Damage
Extra Damage.condition: When you hit a creature or object with a weapon attack on your turn
Extra Damage.activationCondition: When you hit a creature or object with a weapon attack on your turn

Twinned Spell.name: Twinned Spell
Twinned Spell.description:
When you cast a spell that targets only one creature and doesn’t have a range of self, you can spend a number of sorcery points equal to 1 + the spell’s circle to target a second creature in range with the same spell. (Cantrips cost only 1 sorcery point.)

To be eligible, a spell must be incapable of targeting more than one creature at the spell’s current circle. For example, magic missile and scorching ray aren’t eligible but acid arrow and ray of enfeeblement are.

utility.condition: When you cast a spell that targets only one creature and doesn’t have a range of self
utility.activationCondition: When you cast a spell that targets only one creature and doesn’t have a range of self

Unarmored Defense.name: Unarmored Defense
Unarmored Defense.description: While you are wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your DEX modifier + your WIS modifier.

Unarmored Movement.name: Unarmored Movement
Unarmored Movement.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a shield. This bonus increases when you reach certain monk levels, as shown in the Monk Progression table.

Unarmored Movement.title: Unarmored Movement
Unarmored Movement.hint: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a shield.

Uncanny Dodge.name: Uncanny Dodge
Uncanny Dodge.description: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to reduce the attack’s damage against you by half.

utility.condition: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack
utility.activationCondition: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack

Unstoppable.name: Unstoppable
Unstoppable.description: When you start your turn, you can choose to end one of the following conditions affecting you: blinded, charmed, frightened, incapacitated, paralyzed, or stunned. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your PB, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

End Condition.name: End Condition
End Condition.description: You can end one of the following conditions affecting you: blinded, charmed, frightened, incapacitated, paralyzed, or stunned.

Unyielding Might.name: Unyielding Might
Unyielding Might.description:
You have perfected the application of might and mettle. You gain the following benefits:

  • When you make an ability check or save using STR or CON, treat any d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

  • Your melee weapon attacks deal additional damage, equal to your STR score, to objects and structures.

Valiant Inspiration.name: Valiant Inspiration
Valiant Inspiration.description: When a creature with one of your Bardic Inspiration dice successfully deals damage with an attack, the creature can expend and roll the die to deal extra damage equal to the number rolled. The extra damage is of the same type as that of the triggering attack (though the attacker chooses which type if there are more than one). Alternatively, when a creature with one of your Bardic Inspiration dice takes damage, the creature can use its reaction to expend and roll the die to reduce the amount of damage taken by an amount equal to the number rolled + your CHA modifier.

Visions of Distant Realms.name: Visions of Distant Realms
Visions of Distant Realms.description: You can cast the arcane eye spell as often as you wish, without expending a spell slot or material components.

War Domain.name: War Domain
War Domain.description: Gods of the War Domain celebrate strength, reveling in the glory of victory and the thrill of competition. Gods with evil or chaotic proclivities emphasize the destruction and violence of war, but such gods are primarily worshipped by villainous factions. Most societies and heroic characters worship war gods who honor courage, demand excellence on the field of battle, and reward those who take up arms when the need is great.
War Domain.short: Gods of the War Domain celebrate strength, reveling in the glory of victory and the thrill of competition. Gods with evil or chaotic proclivities emphasize the destruction and violence of war, but such gods are primarily worshipped by villainous factions. Most societies and heroic characters worship war gods who honor courage, demand excellence on the field of battle, and reward those who take up arms when the need is great.

War Domain Spells.name: War Domain Spells
War Domain Spells.description:
You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the War Domain Spells table. See the Cleric Subclass class feature for how these spells work.

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.rules.JournalEntry.PBo9j135EucIDliX.JournalEntryPage.WjXj1rpzbhcIUYMg inline]

Warlock.name: Warlock
Warlock.short: Supernatural casters who draw magic from Wyrd forces.

Water.name: Water
Water.description:
You gain a 90-foot swimming speed and can breathe underwater. You are resistant to cold and poison damage. You gain the following action:

  • Wave (Recharge 4–6). Each creature within 5 feet of you must make a STR save (DC 10 + your PB). On a failure, a target takes 2d8 + your PB bludgeoning damage. If a target is Large or smaller, it is also grappled (escape DC 14). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained and unable to breathe unless it can breathe in water. On a successful STR save, a creature takes half damage and isn’t grappled. At the start of each of your turns, each target grappled by you takes 2d8 + your PB bludgeoning damage. A creature within 5 feet of a grappled target can pull it out of the grapple if it expends an action to attempt a DC 14 STR (Athletics) check—freeing the target on a success.

Wave.name: Wave
Wave.description: Each creature within 5 feet of you must make a [[/save]]. On a failure, a target takes [[/damage]] damage. If a target is Large or smaller, it is also grappled (escape DC 14). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained and unable to breathe unless it can breathe in water. On a successful STR save, a creature takes half damage and isn’t grappled. At the start of each of your turns, each target grappled by you takes [[/damage]] damage. A creature within 5 feet of a grappled target can pull it out of the grapple if it expends an action to attempt a [[/check strength athletics 14]] check—freeing the target on a success.

Weapon Master.name: Weapon Master
Weapon Master.description: Most fighters feel a deep connection to their weapons, but none more so than the Weapon Master. For them, a favored bow or blade is a natural extension of their body and a trusted ally. Fighters who pursue this subclass learn to use their chosen arms with efficiency and artistry.
Weapon Master.short: Most fighters feel a deep connection to their weapons, but none more so than the Weapon Master. For them, a favored bow or blade is a natural extension of their body and a trusted ally. Fighters who pursue this subclass learn to use their chosen arms with efficiency and artistry.

Whispers of the Grave.name: Whispers of the Grave
Whispers of the Grave.description: You can perform the speak with dead ritual as often as you wish, without a ritual book or material components.

Wholeness of Body.name: Wholeness of Body
Wholeness of Body.description: When you roll initiative and have at least 1 hit point, you regain a number of hit points equal to your CON modifier + your monk level.

healing.condition: When you roll initiative and have at least 1 hit point
healing.activationCondition: When you roll initiative and have at least 1 hit point

Wild Shape.name: Wild Shape
Wild Shape.description:
You gain the ability to channel energy directly from nature to produce effects beyond spells. You start with two effects: Beast Form and Draw Power. Some subclasses grant additional effects as you advance in levels, as noted in their descriptions.

When you use your Wild Shape feature, simply choose which specific effect you want, such as Beast Form. Some of these effects require a save, and in those cases, the DC is equal to your druid spell save DC.

Beginning at 2nd level, you can use your Wild Shape feature once until you finish a short or long rest, then twice between rests at 6th level, three times at 13th level, and four times at 18th level. When you finish a short or long rest, you regain all expended uses.

Wild Shape: Beast Form

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.classes.Item.jkERtjTXGWjrVx4X inline]

Wild Shape: Draw Power

@Embed[Compendium.black-flag.classes.Item.gHJSfQF6PM7JxQ5Z inline]

Wild Shape: Beast Form.name: Wild Shape: Beast Form
Wild Shape: Beast Form.description:
As an action, you can magically assume the form of a Beast. When you first gain this feature, choose two Beasts of challenge rating (CR) 1/4 or less as your known forms. To select a known form, you must have seen or interacted with a Beast in your past. For example, a druid that hails from a desert environment is unlikely to be familiar enough with a sea-dwelling creature like a dolphin to select it as a known form. The Beast you choose can’t have any legendary actions or lair actions.

As you advance in druid levels, you can choose more beast forms as shown in the Beast Forms Known column of the Druid Progression table.

When you gain a level of druid, you can replace one known form with a different form of your choosing. Any new form you choose must abide by the form restrictions.

Wild Shape: Draw Power.name: Wild Shape: Draw Power
Wild Shape: Draw Power.description:
As a bonus action, you can reshape the natural energies around you to draw them within yourself and recharge your spellcasting ability. When you do so, choose a single expended Primordial spell slot to recover. The recovered spell slot must be of a circle equal to or less than your proficiency bonus (PB). For example, a 5th-level druid with a PB of +3 could recover an expended 1st, 2nd, or 3rd-circle Primordial spell slot with this feature.

You can’t use Draw Power while transformed by the Beast Form effect of your Wild Shape feature, even if you have multiple uses of Wild Shape available to expend.

Wind Up.name: Wind Up
Wind Up.description:
Requires Wielding a Heavy or a Versatile Melee Weapon with Both Hands

As a bonus action, you ready a powerful attack against a nearby target. Select one target you can see within 10 feet of you. If you hit that target with a melee weapon attack before the end of your turn, the first such attack deals extra damage equal to your PB (of the same damage type as the weapon).

utility.description: Select one target you can see within 10 feet of you. If you hit that target with a melee weapon attack before the end of your turn, the first such attack deals extra damage equal to your PB (of the same damage type as the weapon).

Witch Sight.name: Witch Sight
Witch Sight.description: You can see the true form of any creature with the Shapechanger tag or creature concealed by illusion or transmutation magic while the creature is within 30 feet of you and within line of sight.

Wizard.name: Wizard
Wizard.short: Cerebral casters who wield Arcane magic.

Wrestler's Clutch.name: Wrestler's Clutch
Wrestler's Clutch.description:
Requires a Melee Weapon

When you hit a creature with a melee attack, the creature must succeed on a [[/save]] save or become grappled by you. While this grapple persists, stunts you use against the grappled creature don't expend uses of your Stunts feature. This grapple ends if you attack another creature, the target escapes, or you end it (no action required).

savingThrow.condition: When you hit a creature with a melee attack
savingThrow.activationCondition: When you hit a creature with a melee attack
